chunks.text: ORDER SHEET HIGH COURT OF SINDH CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P No.D-1554 of 2022 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 14.06.2022 Mr. Muhammad Hashim Laghari, advocate for petitioners Mr. Ashfaque Nabi Qazi, Assistant Attorney General Mr. Ayaz Ali Rajper, Assistant A.G Sindh Senior Law Officer ECP (Abdullah Hanjra), Law Officer ECP (Zaheer Abbas) & District Election Commissioner Sanghar (Shahnawaz Brohi) *** The grievance of the petitioners is that Deh Jampur has been wrongly delimited and should be shifted from Union Council No.49 to Union Council No.50. He had filed objections to that effect, which resulted in impugned order dated 21.03.2022, passed by the Regional Election Commissioner/Delimitation Authority Shaheed Benazirabad, whereby his petition was rejected by observation given in paragraph-3 of the impugned order, which reads as under:- “3. The Delimitation Committee, Sanghar submitted its comments in the matter. The Union Council No.49 has population 22764 and dehs have compactness with each other. The Dehs of Taluka Jam Nawaz Ali have meagre population. Therefore, many adjoining dehs are placed in Union Councils. Furthermore, population of all Union Councils of Taluka Jam Nawaz Ali have uniformity which may be verified from Form-8. It is not possible to include Deh Jampur in Union Council No.50.” We find that Regional Election Commission/Delimitation Authority has attended to the objections raised by the petitioners and has carried out the delimitation fully in accordance with Section 20 of the Election Act, 2017, which relates to principles of delimitation, as such the petition is dismissed.

Furthermore, population of all Union Councils of Taluka Jam Nawaz Ali have uniformity which may be verified from Form-8. It is not possible to include Deh Jampur in Union Council No.50.” We find that Regional Election Commission/Delimitation Authority has attended to the objections raised by the petitioners and has carried out the delimitation fully in accordance with Section 20 of the Election Act, 2017, which relates to principles of delimitation, as such the petition is dismissed. JUDGE JUDGE Sajjad Ali Jessar
